steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim states Claudine has a “strong emotional attachment to the surrounding forests.” The correct quotation must show personal, emotional language about the forests — not just observation or description.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "If I had a Mamma, I know very well that she would not have let me stay [in Montigny] twenty-four hours. But Papa—he doesn't notice anything and doesn't bother about me.": Discusses her relationship with her parents, not the forests. ✗
  • Choice B — "No small creatures in those great woods; no tall grasses; but beaten earth, now dry, and sonorous, now soft on account of the springs. Rabbits with white scats [tails] range through them and timid deer who run so fast that you can only guess their passage.": Observational description of the woods — no emotional language. ✗
  • Choice C — "And then there are my favorites, the great woods that are sixteen and twenty years old. It makes my heart bleed to see one of those cut down.": “My favorites” shows personal connection; “makes my heart bleed” is explicit emotional distress at their loss. ✓
  • Choice D — "Green meadows make rifts in [the woods] here and there, so do little patches of cultivation. But these do not amount to much, for the magnificent woods devour everything. As a result, this lovely region is atrociously poor and its few scattered farms.": Describes the woods' dominance of the landscape but does not reveal Claudine's personal feelings about them. ✗
That is the correct answer.
